---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# ggrefine <a href="https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggrefine/"><img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="139" alt="ggrefine website" /></a>


<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of ggrefine is to ...

## Installation

You can install the development version of ggrefine from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("davidhodge931/ggrefine")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

```{r example}
library(ggrefine)
## basic example code
```
